DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-15-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Douglas C. Fladseth, Esq. (707-545-2600) appears telephonically as counsel for plaintiffs/opposing parties, Melissa Musso James et al.

Attorney, Kevin T. Dunbar of Dunbar & Associtates (562-491-7600) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant/moving party, Koshland Pharmacy

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT KOSHLAND PHARMACY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part WITH leave to amend and in part WITHOUT leave to amend. As to drugs, a pharmacy's services are those of a professional health care provider, so general negligence is not a proper claim in this case. (See Huggins v. Longs Drug Stores California, Inc. (1993) 6 Cal.4th 124, 132; CCP 340.5.) The demurrer to the first cause of action is thus SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. The demurrer to the second cause of action is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, because pharmacies that compound drugs are immune from product liability claims. (Murphy v. E.R. Squibb & Sons, Inc. (1985) 40 Cal.3d 672, 676-81.) Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #59761, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
